title: Post-16 National Insurance contributions (NICs) grant
body: "This guidance is for:    maintained school and academy sixth forms  16 to 19 academies  16 to 19 schools    sixth-form colleges   general further education colleges   designated institutions (including the new designated institutions that form part of higher education provider group structures)   local authorities   We are making approximately £155 million available in financial year 2025 to 2026 to support institutions with the increase to employers’ National Insurance contributions arising from their post-16 education from April 2025.  Additional funding for pre-16 provision will be allocated through the National Insurance contributions (NICs) grant.  Funding for high needs settings is being allocated via the core schools budget grant (CSBG) 2025 to 2026.  Methodology   This document sets out the methodology for the post-16 NICs grant. This includes guidance on how we will allocate funding for financial year 2025 to 2026 for eligible institutions. There are 3 components to the post-16 NICs grant:    16 to 19   non 16 to 19 funded students and apprentices (in eligible settings) local authority centrally employed teachers (CETs) and centrally employed support staff    Settings will only receive the components that apply to their situation. The components are based on the most current data DfE holds to calculate the share of the grant.    Conditions of grant   We have published the conditions of grant  Allocations and payment  We have published the allocations.  We will make one payment to cover the 2025 to 2026 financial year.  Local authorities and maintained school sixth forms and 16 to 19 schools, all colleges and designated institutions will receive payment in September 2025.  Academy sixth forms and 16 to 19 academies will receive payment in October 2025.  "
